comments.body: There’s always value = object instanceof Record ? object.getClass().getDeclaredMethod(property).invoke(object) : BeanUtils.getProperty(object, property);

Your answer could be improved with additional supporting information. Please edit to add further details, such as citations or documentation, so that others can confirm that your answer is correct. You can find more information on how to write good answers in the help center.

answers.body:
You can use a BeanIntrospector for records:

public final class RecordBeanIntrospector implements BeanIntrospector {

   /** the single instance */
   public static final RecordBeanIntrospector INSTANCE = new RecordBeanIntrospector();

   static {
      PropertyUtils.addBeanIntrospector(RecordBeanIntrospector.INSTANCE);
   }

   private RecordBeanIntrospector() {
      // only one instance
   }

   public static synchronized void init() {
      // does nothing, but by calling it we make sure that the class is loaded and the instance added to PropertyUtils
   }

   @Override
   public void introspect(final IntrospectionContext context) throws IntrospectionException {
      final Class<?> clazz = context.getTargetClass();
      if (clazz.isRecord()) {
         for (final RecordComponent rc : clazz.getRecordComponents()) {
            final PropertyDescriptor pd = new PropertyDescriptor(rc.getName(), rc.getAccessor(), null);
            context.addPropertyDescriptor(pd);
         }
      }
   }

}

title: BeanUtils getProperty not working for record
body:
If my object is a record this is not working :

value = BeanUtils.getProperty(object, property);

I guess it is looking for a getter which does not exist.

Is there an alternative for java 17, that will work for classes and record ?
